ScenarioText: Its lunch time. What should you eat?
Choice1Description: A cooked simple meal from home - made by mum.
Choice1Feedback: You brought lunch from home - smart move and still affordable for daily meals.
Choice2Description: Standard bowl of noodles - nothing special.
Choice2Feedback: A balanced choice that fits a regular student budget without overextending.
Choice3Feedback: Tasty option but at this rate your food money will run out before month end.

ScenarioText: How will you get to school today?
Choice1Description: No transport needed, let's be healthy.
Choice1Feedback: Great way to save and stay active - every dollar counts.
Choice2Description: Public transport is reliable and efficient.
Choice2Feedback: Good planning - buses are cheap and reliable for getting around daily.
Choice3Description: A quick door to door ride.
Choice3Feedback: Convenient for rushing but this is heavy spending for one journey - avoid making it a habit.

ScenarioText: Your friends are hanging out after school. Join them?
Choice1Feedback: Responsible choice and your future self will thank you for sticking to your plan.
Choice2Description: Free time chatting at nearby block.
Choice2Feedback: Best of both worlds you stay connected without spending a single cent.
Choice3Feedback: Its fun but adding this weekly will drain your wallet quickly so be careful.

ScenarioText: Mid week check. How is your savings going?
Choice1Description: Move money to your savings pot.
Choice1Feedback: Good discipline in the middle of the week - building this habit early is beneficial.
Choice2Description: Take a break spend nothing today.
Choice2Feedback: Excellent reset day after earlier spending - this protects your monthly goal.
Choice3Name: Why Save When I Can Enjoy?
Choice3Feedback: One small treat is fine but thinking like this every few days means your money disappears fast so be careful with this mindset.

ScenarioText: Its raining. Whats your transport choice?
Choice1Feedback: You stayed dry and saved money - thats good planning even in bad weather.
Choice2Feedback: Reasonable cost for comfort and safety on a rainy day is a fair trade-off.
Choice3Feedback: Getting home dry is nice but at this cost you're prioritizing convenience over money.

ScenarioText: Its Saturday. What should you do for fun?
Choice1Description: Free or cheap games on phone
Choice1Feedback: Low cost fun that lets you chill without spending much on a weekend.
Choice2Feedback: Free social time in neighborhood is the best budget win for weekend fun.
Choice3Feedback: Malls are fun but theres hidden spending on food and impulse buys so watch out.

ScenarioText: End of week reflection. Do you deserve a treat?
Choice1Feedback: Strong finish to the week you prioritized your future over a treat right now.
Choice2Feedback: A small reward is okay after a disciplined week but keep it simple and planned.
Choice3Description: Enjoy movies and popcorn with friends.
Choice3Feedback: Rewards are good but big spending should happen only if youve stuck to budget consistently.
